name: open-source-launch
description: Launch an open source project that people actually use. Map the code, write the docs, review for quality, plan the marketing, and produce a launch checklist.

config.instruction: Map the project: structure, entry points, public API surface, dependencies, test coverage. Identify what's ready for public consumption and what needs cleanup.
Write everything an open source project needs: README with quick start, installation, usage examples, API reference, contributing guide, code of conduct, and changelog.
Review for open source readiness: no hardcoded secrets, no internal references, license compliance, consistent code style, clear error messages, good naming.
Plan the launch: Hacker News post, Reddit posts (relevant subreddits), Twitter/X thread, Dev.to article, Product Hunt listing. Write drafts for each. Time the launches for maximum visibility.
Build the launch day checklist: repo public, docs deployed, CI green, license added, social posts scheduled, Show HN draft ready, monitoring set up for issues/stars/forks.
